creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text: After discarding the saved state of my Windows10 VM, I was able to boot it, though it still would not shutdown cleanly. Only available window sizes only the standard, fixed sizes where like 1280x1024. Stretching he window just centered the 180x1024 in the enlarged area. I tried different display drivers and it suddenly stated working properly after I restored the original driver, VBoxSVGA. I have no idea what magic "fixed" the problems, but both Windows10 and Linux now shut down cleanly. As a result, I am closing the ticket, though I don't feel very good about it.
